query: fluorescent overhead lighting institutional office interior unflattering harsh
message: anti_aesthetics/lighting: flat lighting — fluorescent institutional interiors, depressing offices/corridors, depthless cold light

response.objective_caption: An empty institutional corridor with purple-blue walls and matching patterned carpet extends in one-point perspective toward a distant, warmly lit opening. A row of rectangular recessed ceiling lights runs down the center of a white drop ceiling. Several doorways line the left wall, some revealing pale blue interior light, while the far end of the hallway opens into a space with amber-brown walls and ceiling. The corridor appears clean and modern, with vertical wall paneling and bright illumination from the overhead fixtures.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: An empty institutional corridor with walls and carpet bathed in a sickly, unnatural purple-blue cast extends in one-point perspective toward a distant, warmly lit opening. A row of rectangular recessed ceiling lights runs down the center of a white drop ceiling. Several doorways line the left wall, some revealing pale interior light, while the far end opens into an amber-brown space. The pervasive unhealthy tint shifts all surfaces toward a contaminated hue, while the sterile, deserted hallway and unnatural coloration evoke a heavy atmosphere of unease and quiet dread.
